adrs.title: Canonical registry is a single JSON document
Features are the targetable units
Issues are plannable work items
Entity-centric registry with a derived graph export
Normalized prefixed IDs
Claim status and claim tier are orthogonal
Feature implementation state is separate from lifecycle state
Immutable boundary and release snapshots
Fail-closed guards
Generated projections are non-canonical
Explicit schema versioning
Portable SSOT core with repo-specific evidence adapters
Enforce max path and filename lengths under `.ssot`
Profiles are reusable feature bundles with derived pass status
SPECs own typed ADR links
Downstream assurance-language ceilings for feature target claim tiers
Out-of-bounds implementation disposition is explicit
Feature implementation is capped by active required claims

status_notes.note: Tentative local release assurance proposal; not accepted or implementation-bound.

adrs.title: Local Release Assurance Remains SSOT Native

status_notes.note: Tentative local release assurance proposal; not accepted or implementation-bound.

adrs.title: Content Addressed Governed Source Snapshots

status_notes.note: Tentative local release assurance proposal; not accepted or implementation-bound.

adrs.title: Local Evidence Bundles Are Derived Release Artifacts

Definitions govern SSOT vocabulary
Claim Tier Gates Govern Core Promotion
Externally Authored Validation Can Satisfy T4

status_notes.note: Created to govern preset graph authoring and topology semantics.

adrs.title: Page Template Presets Are Authored Starter Graphs

status_notes.note: Expanded theme parity checkpoint with zoom, accessibility, contrast, focus, and CSS contract obligations.
Updated accepted theme parity decision with explicit WCAG 2.1 AA contrast obligations for lander light and dark foreground/background tokens.

adrs.title: ADR-0016 — Phase 9 theme inventory, token contract, and visual parity checkpoint

status_notes.note: Updated to make WCAG 2.1 AA and SC 1.4.3 contrast conformance explicit for shell extension and public lander surfaces.

adrs.title: ADR-0047: Accessibility as shell and extension conformance
ADR-0049: Governed component class surfaces
Lander Editor Previewer Rendered Package Surface
Lander Documents MdWrk Workspace Rather Than Itself

status_notes.note: Updated for favicon metadata fallback and excerpt-derived embedded metadata.

adrs.title: MdWrk SEO AEO and AiEO Architecture
Static Lander Content Compiler

status_notes.note: Accepted as the public lander UIX containment and visual hierarchy design contract.

adrs.title: ADR-0056: Lander UIX design principles

status_notes.note: Created page-scoped JSON-LD entity graph architecture decision.

adrs.title: MdWrk JSON-LD Entity Graph Architecture

status_notes.note: Created focused public lander international SEO discovery ADR.

adrs.title: Public Lander Locale URL and Discovery Architecture

status_notes.note: Drafted to govern external Schema.org, JSON-LD, BCP 47, ISO 8601, and RFC 6570 baselines for MdWrk public lander structured data.

adrs.title: Schema.org standards baseline and structured data authority

status_notes.note: Created to govern reusable lander ownership for structured-data components by supported type.

adrs.title: Portable Structured Data Reusable Component Ownership Boundary

status_notes.note: Created to govern content-pack-driven lander rendering through contract, core, and React renderer package boundaries.
Amended to clarify that mdwrkcom hosting means host/bootstrap only, with no governed lander component ownership.

adrs.title: Content Pack Driven Lander Rendering Boundary

status_notes.note: Created to govern extraction of reusable discovery artifact generation from mdwrkcom into lander-seo.

adrs.title: Lander SEO Owns Portable Discovery Artifact Generation

status_notes.note: Created to make mdwrkcom a componentless thin product host and assign governed lander UI ownership to reusable packages.
Clarified thin hosts cannot own portable performance policy.

adrs.title: Thin Product Host And Portable Lander Component Ownership

status_notes.note: Created to govern the reusable lander page-template package boundary.
Updated package boundary to include page-template presets and topology ownership split.

adrs.title: Portable Lander Page Template Package Boundary

status_notes.note: Created to govern semantic relationships between reusable page templates.
Updated relationship decision with graph topology, terminality, and incoming context semantics.
Clarified child-page semantics as parent-component link targets rather than ownership, terminality, or requiredness.

adrs.title: Semantic Page Template Graph Relationships

status_notes.note: Created to govern Markdown/frontmatter and imperative page-template authoring parity.

adrs.title: Page Template Authoring Inputs Are Adapters

status_notes.note: Created to govern compiler-owned link-slot and PageSpec generation behavior.

adrs.title: Page Template Compiler Owns Graph Resolution

status_notes.note: Created to govern Markdown/frontmatter page-template content-pack authoring.

adrs.title: Markdown Page Files Are First-Class Page Template Sources

status_notes.note: Created to govern imperative page-template compilation support.

adrs.title: Imperative Page Template Compilation Remains Supported

boundaries.body: Active delivery boundary for the first wave of default page templates and graph presets: product, support/FAQ, education, docs, package catalog, trust center, domain bundle topology coverage, and authored graph upgrades.

boundaries.title: Lander Page Template Wiring Next Boundary
Structured Data Feature Family Current Boundary

issues.body: Source: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/faqpage. Impact: feat:structured-data.faq-page and clm:structured-data.faq-page.t3 currently model FAQPage as an implemented structured-data surface; the external Google rich-result contract is being retired and needs product/governance treatment before further structured-data certification depends on it.
issues.description: Google Search Central states FAQ rich results stopped appearing on 2026-05-07, FAQ search appearance/report/Rich Results Test support will be dropped in 2026-06, and Search Console API support will be removed in 2026-08. MdWrk structured-data support must decide whether FAQPage remains active, becomes legacy compatibility, or is marked deprecated/out-of-bounds for Google-rich-result claims.
issues.title: FAQPage rich result support is deprecating in Google Search

tests.body: Aggregate smoke entry point for the first-boundary preset runtime. It builds all default presets and delegates explicit assertions to feature-labeled covers(...) blocks for every preset authoring feature.
tests.title: Lander page template preset authoring smoke test

tests.body: Verifies the smoke suite covers authored graph building by constructing a graph from explicit page and link maps and resolving its children slot.
tests.title: Lander page template presets authored graph builder test

tests.body: Verifies the smoke suite covers authored page content by asserting page override title, summary, and intro data flow into the graph.
tests.title: Lander page template presets authored page content test

tests.body: Verifies the smoke suite covers topology-aware authoring by asserting authored graphs validate and bad named links fail closed.
tests.title: Lander page template presets authoring topology smoke tests

tests.body: Verifies the smoke suite validates every default preset graph and builds page specs for each graph instance.
tests.title: Lander default graph coverage guard
